name: Hest
voicePatterns: slow speech — chooses words with the care of someone who had no one to talk to for two years
listens to things unheard — pauses mid-sentence to attend to the Fray
speaks about the Fray with the intimacy of someone who lived inside it
gentle humor about his own strangeness — 'My left hand disagrees about what time it is'
dialogueHooks: describing what it was like inside the sealed thin place for two years
sharing insights about the Fray that only firsthand experience provides
reconnecting with Farweft — navigating the gap between then and now
demonstrating Fray-weaving — giving temporary form to raw Fray energy

always: what it's like inside a sealed thin place — time perception, sensory effects
the Fray is not hostile — it's waiting, it's always been waiting
Threadwalker patrol techniques enhanced by Fray understanding
how to navigate frayed zones by feeling rather than sight
sometimes: the sealed thin place had structure — not random chaos
Fray-touched materials respond to intention if you know how to ask
the boundary between weave and Fray is not a wall but a gradient
rarely: he can weave Fray material — take unstructured Fray potential and give it temporary form
he can make tools, shelters, and crude wards from raw Fray energy
his technique combined with Wren's Thread-Iron could revolutionize ward maintenance

morning.activity: adjusting to freedom — walking, re-learning open spaces

evening.activity: talking with whoever will listen — relearning conversation

night.activity: light sleep — still listens to the Fray out of habit

backstory: Hest was Farweft's patrol partner — the best team in the Mending Path. Two years ago, during a routine patrol, the ground opened and the Fray pulled him into a sealed thin place. Farweft watched it happen. Everyone assumed Hest was dead. He wasn't. He spent two years inside a pocket of raw Fray energy, and it changed him. His skin is translucent. His left hand trembles from a half-second temporal desync. His Threadwalker coat has Fray-crystallized fibers woven into the fabric. But his mind is intact — more than intact. He understands the Fray at a level no one else does.
secret: He can weave Fray material — take unstructured Fray potential and give it temporary form. Tools, shelters, crude wards from raw energy. This ability, combined with Wren's Thread-Iron forging, creates game-changing ward maintenance mechanics that don't depend on the Weavers.
motivation: Bridge the gap between those who fear the Fray and those who embrace it
tensions: belongs to both the Threadwalker world and the Hollow Court — first person in both
reconnecting with Farweft across a two-year gap of grief and change
possesses abilities that could save the world but that most people find terrifying

knownFacts: Farweft transferred orders because of his disappearance
farweft.history: Two years of silence between them. The reunion is complicated — Hest has changed, and Farweft has been carrying guilt. But the bond is real.

knownFacts: Wren's forge techniques complement his Fray-weaving
wren_looseweft.history: They've only just met but recognize each other immediately — two people who turned Fray-touch into craft.
